summarization.a: The figure illustrates two modes of fluid dynamics: diffusive mode and dynamic mode. It includes a conceptual diagram of the primary and secondary jets, and heatmaps representing concentration distributions.
summarization.b: The line charts depict the temporal evolution of normalized concentration (c/c0) at different Reynolds numbers (Re) for four quadrants (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4).

data_extraction.b:
|Reynolds number | Graph look|
|---|---|
|40|Smooth distribution over time|
|80|Smooth distribution over time|
|200| Smooth distribution over time|
|800|Non-smooth distribution over time|
|2400|Non-smooth distribution over time|

a.question: Which flow mode results in distribution of reactant over the full wafer?
What color depicts a higher concentration of reactants?

b.question: How can turbulance be observed with the graph for Re = 2400?
b.answer: b	The lines show peaks and dips instead of a smooth graph, indicating there is turbulance.

b.question: Why is the flow lower in Q3?
b.answer: b	Q3 is seperated from the influx of species by the grid support, resulting in less direct flow. The flow in Q3 is diffused there.
